programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo want to play games on the trampoline with Dad; Dad, Bluey and Bingo head to the dump to throw away their rubbish; Bluey and Bingo play pretend zoo.

programme.title: Muppet Babies: The Karate Cub/Don't Over Duet
programme.desc: Fozzie must learn some 'Camilla-rate' in order to join the martial arts team, The Kickin' Chickens. Piggy practices her performance for Miss Nanny too hard and loses her voice!

programme.title: Disney Junior Ready for Preschool: Clean
programme.desc: Disney music shorts to help children prepare for preschool.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: Ye Olde Royal Heist/ Tea Time Trouble!
programme.desc: Mickey and Minnie celebrate their raceversary in London, but things get complicated when the Queen's stolen Royal Ruby ends up in Mickey's roadster.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Mixed-Up Adventures: Caution: Kids at Work! / The Snoozy Doozy Pet and Breakfast!
programme.desc: Buddy is Mickey's number one fan, and he can't wait to get to work for Mini=Mechanics day. Then, The Happy Helpers need to transform the Snoozy-Doozy into a pet hotel when they get so many pet-sitting requests.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Lost Lovey/ Diggity Dog
programme.desc: Mia loses Lovey, so Pip and Freddy go on a wild chase to find it. The Fliers' delivery is in jeopardy when their puppy student becomes the master at digging.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Art for Pug's Sake/ Winter Wonder-Pug
programme.desc: The pugs try to hang something of Bob's in a famous Italian art museum. Bingo and Rolly look for the first snowflake of the season while helping other animals prepare for winter.

programme.title: Vampirina: The Boo Boys Are Back/ Pixie Problem
programme.desc: The Ghoul Girls and Poltergeist Pat perform at the Paranormal Pop Festival. A pixie family is visiting the B&B!

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: The Fearful Flier/ Lend Me Your Paw
programme.desc: Bodhi faces his fear to earn his final stamp towards becoming a Super Flier. After Mia gets a thorn stuck in her paw, she worries it will hurt when K.C. tries to take it out.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Mixed-Up Adventures: Campy Camper Day!/ Founder's Day Flounder!
programme.desc: While on a camping trip, Mickey, Donald and Daisy can't unplug from their electronic devices. Mickey and the gang celebrate the 100th birthday of Hot Dog Hills.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: Mickey's Perfecto Day!/ Running of the Roadsters!
programme.desc: While in Madrid, Mickey plans a perfect day for Minnie, but a playful baby bull has other plans. Minnie and Daisy make a bet with Mickey and Donald over who will win the race.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Duck, Duck, Dog / Mr. Bob Goes to Washington
programme.desc: After meeting a duckling that thinks he's a puppy, the pugs must teach him to be a duck. Bob and the pups head to Washington to compete in an environmental invention competition.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Keia's New Doghouse / The Fang Fairy
programme.desc: After Bob builds a doghouse for Keia, the pugs encourage her to host a puppy housewarming party. Rolly loses his first puppy tooth and can't wait for a visit from the Fang Fairy!

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Puggy-ology/ Squirrels Just Wanna Have Fun
programme.desc: The pugs travel to an archeology site to find artifacts for Bob's sister. Then, when a noisy squirrel keeps Bob from getting work done, the pugs must work together to get the squirrel back to its family.

programme.title: PJ Masks: Owlette and the Flash Flip Trip/ Catboy and the Pogo Dozer
programme.desc: When Romeo erases all the stories from books, Owlette enlists the help of Flossy Flash. Romeo uses boxing gloves to break into the museum and steal a jetpack.

programme.title: PJ Masks: Romeo's Melody/PJ Robot Takes Control
programme.desc: The PJ Masks must wear ear-protecting helmets when Romeo writes a song that turns listeners into his servant. PJ Robot tries to turn Romeo's Flying Factory into a Flying Party!

programme.title: Muppet Babies: The Great Muppet Sport-a-Thon/ You Say Potato, I Say Best Friend
programme.desc: The gang puts on a sporting competition but Piggy keeps losing! The Muppet Babies are confused when Gonzo becomes best friends with a potato.

programme.title: Muppet Babies: Kermit Levels Up/ Frogs of a Feather
programme.desc: Fozzie's Funny Joke Show gets interrupted after Kermit finds a video game. When a new friend, Carlos the Frog, visits, Fozzie assumes he likes all the same things as Kermit.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Hissy's Big Day/ Go, Dog. Go!
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly try to cheer Hissy up by taking her to their favourite places. Later, A.R.F. is reprogrammed to take the pugs where they want, but not being them back.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: To the Library!/ARF's Birthday
programme.desc: When Bob discovers he lost a library book, the Pugs race out on a mission to get it before it’s due. The Pugs go on a last minute mission for party supplies for ARF’s birthday.

programme.title: PJ Masks: Owlette and the Moonflower/ Slowpoke Gekko
programme.desc: Luna Girl uses her Luna Beam to create a giant evil moonflower. If Gekko doesn't learn how to stop being distracted he won't be able to stop Romeo.

programme.title: PJ Masks: Moonfizzle Balls/ Soccer Ninjalinos
programme.desc: Catboy works a bit too fast to kick back Luna Girl’s moonfizzle balls. Owlette learns that being part of the team means coaching Catboy and Gekko to defeat Night Ninja.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Adopt-a-Palooza/ The Legend of Captain Wunderbark
programme.desc: When a puppy gets lost at Adopt-A-Palooza, it's up to the pugs, Keia and their new friend to find it. Bingo, Rolly and Keia go off in search of Captain Wunderbark's long-lost ship.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Art for Pug's Sake/ Winter Wonder-Pug
programme.desc: The pugs try to hang something of Bob's in a famous Italian art museum. Bingo and Rolly look for the first snowflake of the season while helping other animals prepare for winter.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Donald's Clubhouse
programme.desc: Mickey leaves Donald in charge of the Clubhouse. But he and Goofy accidentally turn a small mess into a big mess, so the whole gang must work together to tidy up in time for a visit from Mother Goose, Clarabelle and her trio of singing chickens.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Minnie and Daisy's Flower Shower
programme.desc: With the help of Professor Von Drake's special Pitter Patter Glitter, Minnie and Daisy turn normal clouds into rain clouds so they can water their flower gardens in time for the big flower show.

programme.title: Muppet Babies: Summer Penguin, P.I./ You Ought to Be in Pictures
programme.desc: Summer assumes the role of detective when a heated game of checkers turns unpleasant. While on a field trip to a local art museum, Fozzie's lunchbox is stolen by a mischievous monkey and the Muppet Babies must chase him through paintings to get it back.

programme.title: Muppet Babies: Hatastrophe/ Fly South
programme.desc: Kermit accidentally loses Fozzie's new hat; Once Gonzo learns that birds fly south for the winter, he tries to keep his friends from leaving.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: Super-Charged: The Big Cheesy / Shenannygans!
programme.desc: During the Cooper's Hill Cheese Rolling Chase, a cheese wheel spins out of control! The Happy Helpers work as nannies for two children in London and take them on new experiences!

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: The Hiking Honeybees
programme.desc: Mickey and the gang participate in high-spirited races around the globe.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Goofy Goes Goofy
programme.desc: Goofy gets cloned after getting sprayed by Prof. Von Drake's latest experimental goo.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Donald's Brand New Clubhouse
programme.desc: When Donald complains everyone is in his way, Mickey and pals help him build his own clubhouse.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Mickey's Silly Problem
programme.desc: When the Clubhouse's Silly Switch gets stuck on silly, everything turns silly.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Oh, Toodles!
programme.desc: Professor Von Drake is working on a surprise invention when he discovers that he is missing four of his very special tools: a shape-wrench, a hammer, a paintbrush and a whistle.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Prince Pete's Catnap
programme.desc: The Clubhouse pals discover what has been keeping Prince Pete awake, after trying hard to help him fall asleep for a nap. It was a stack of peas under the cushion of his favourite car.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Sea Captain Mickey
programme.desc: When Professor Von Drake’s sonar-detecting machine discovers that there’s something big at the bottom of Mickey Lake, Mickey and the gang hop into the Clubhouse Submarine and set out to find the 'Big Something'.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Secret Spy Daisy
programme.desc: A thief tries to steal Clarabelle's secret raisin cookies recipe.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Sir Goofs-a-lot
programme.desc: Goofy sets out on a quest to retrieve three items in order to be knighted the bravest.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Mickey's Big Surprise
programme.desc: Mickey presents his Fun Puzzles.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: Donald's Garage/ Artful Helpers
programme.desc: While Mickey is away, Donald is left in charge and tries to improve the garage. The Happy Helpers are asked to guard a valuable painting at the Hot Dog Hills' Fancy Art Museum.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: Mouse vs. Machine/ Grandpa Beagle's Day Out
programme.desc: Mickey challenges Professor Von Drake's Self-Racing Racer to a race and Billy Beagle asks the Happy Helpers to help his grandpa with some errands.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: The Roadsterettes! / Oh Happy Day!
programme.desc: Famous race car driver Pippa Powers visits Hot Dog Hills for a reunion with her old car club. The Happy Helpers must show Mr. McSnorter how to relax on vacation.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: Agent Double-O-Goof/ Egg-xasperating!
programme.desc: While everyone prepares for the Queen of England to come for tea, Goofy is mistaken for a secret agent in charge of delivering a top-secret briefcase. The Happy Helpers are charged with watching Clara Cluck's egg while she runs errands.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: Hot Dog Daze Afternoon/ Super Sweet Helpers
programme.desc: On Mickey's day off, he continues getting called back to help at the garage. The Happy Helpers look after Mrs. Sweetums' candy store and factory.

programme.title: Bluey: Trampoline/ The Dump/ Zoo
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo want to play games on the trampoline with Dad; Dad, Bluey and Bingo head to the dump to throw away their rubbish; Bluey and Bingo play pretend zoo.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Land of the Rising Pup / ARF-CHOO
programme.desc: The pups travel to Japan to get help from the local snow monkeys and bring back snow to save Bob's ski trip.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: A New Pup in Town / The Last Pup-icorn
programme.desc: When Chloe's new puppy, Keia, runs away, the pups must find her before Chloe returns! When Chloe wishes she could have a unicorn, Bingo, Rolly and Keia set out to find one.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: The Go-long Retriever/ Pot O' Pugs
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly try to get away from a self-fetching stick that begins chasing them. Then, when Bob says he needs a pot of gold, the pups search for one at the end of a rainbow.

programme.title: Gigantosaurus: The Island/ Don't Cave In
programme.desc: Timid Bill and brave Rocky end up stranded on an island, however Rocky suddenly becomes the scared one! Trey warns the little dinos about a cave monster named “Shriekasaurus”.

programme.title: Gigantosaurus: Please Don't Pick The Flowers/ No Joke
programme.desc: Bill accidentally changes Cretacia’s ecosystem when he eats too many rare swamp flowers. Dilo pulls pranks on the dinos!

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: The Grand Food Truck Rally/ Cuckoo La-La
programme.desc: Mickey and his pals race in a grand food truck rally in Paris. While in Paris, the happy helpers help famous fashion designer Cuckoo La-La with a fashion show.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: Daredevil Goofy!/ The Big Broadcast
programme.desc: Goofy gets a chance to compete against a movie hero in a daredevil competition. Then, the happy helpers cover for Billy Beagle as race announcers while he competes.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Cousin Cody/ Hissy's Lost To
programme.desc: The pugs help their nervous puppy cousin, Cody, feel comfortable in a new place. When Hissy’s favorite toy goes missing, the pups go on a mission to find it for their kitty sister.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Free Whaley/ Putting It Together
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly help a baby whale that has been separated from her family. Later, the pugs retrace their steps to remember where they buried Bob's unicycle.

programme.title: PJ Masks: The Wolfy Plan/ The Lizard Theft
programme.desc: Rip tries to gather feathers from Owlette's wings to let the Wolfies fly.

programme.title: PJ Masks: PJ Pinball/ Bounce-a-Tron
programme.desc: The team thwarts Romeo's plan to play a game of pinball in the city with wrecking balls. Catboy wants to prove he is great by stopping Night Ninja on his own.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: International superstars Alvin, Simon and Theodore take a roller coaster ride of music, laughter and stories.

programme.title: Rise of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
programme.desc: The turtles are back and this time they've got awesome powers. Join Ralph, Leo, Donnie and Mikey as they master new weapons, battle absurd mutants and discover a hidden city beneath New York.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: A 10-year-old boy and his fairy godparents have magical adventures.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Sleep Over and Over/ Mother Nature
programme.desc: Chester and AJ are feuding and Timmy is caught in the middle; mom becomes a weathercaster.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Stage Fright/ Gone Flushin'
programme.desc: Vicky decides to be an actress and make Timmy help her land her first role. Dad accidentally flushes Timmy and the fairies down the toilet.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Lights Camera Uh-Oh/ Wax Dave
programme.desc: The Chipmunks have 48 hours to help Jeanette make a student film. But every chipmunk has a very different movie in mind. The Chipmunks follow Dave and find out he's fallen in love with a dangerous woman who wants to turn him into a wax statue.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Back to School/ Bromance
programme.desc: When Dave discovers he didn't graduate from high school, he finds himself in school with Alvin. Simon has found a new best friend, Jamie. But when Jamie realizes that Alvin is the more popular brother, he ditches Simon.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Brittany the Body Snatcher/ Agent Smith
programme.desc: When Alvin notices Brittany being nice to him he's sure an alien has taken over her body. In order to earn a job on the school newspaper, Alvin helps Simon uncover the biggest story the school's ever seen.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Microphony/ Totally Spaced Out
programme.desc: Every parent falls for Vicky's ad campaign; Yugopotamia is conquered by an alien race.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Chip Off the Old Chip/ Snow Bound
programme.desc: Timmy feels his voice won't work in a school play; Timmy and Vicki end up in an avalanche.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Dinklescouts/ I Dream of Cosmo
programme.desc: Timmy wishes Mr. Dinkleberg would replace Dad as Squirrely Scout leader. Dad tries to regain Timmy's respect and his leader position.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Man Ray Returns/ Larry the Floor Manager
programme.desc: When Man Ray rents Squidward's house for the weekend, it's up to SpongeBob and Patrick to stop his evil vacation. Mr. Krabs takes a vacation and makes Larry the Lobster the temporary manager of the Krusty Krab.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Krabby Patty Creature Feature/ Teacher's Pests
programme.desc: A new secret Krabby Patty formula has disastrous results for Bikini Bottom, and only SpongeBob can save the day. Mr. Krabs and Plankton have to take boating school classes.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Boating Buddies/ Krabby Kronicle
programme.desc: Squidward is forced to attend Boating School with SpongeBob.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Mermaidman Begins/ Plankton's Good Eye
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick learn the secret origins of Mermaid Man and Branacle Boy from the heroes themselves.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: SquarePants Family Vacation
programme.desc: While on a family road trip to the Great Barrier Reef, SpongeBob and Patrick get lost and end up on the adventure of a lifetime.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: House Sittin' for Sandy/ Smoothe Jazz at Bikini Bottom
programme.desc: All is quiet while SpongeBob is house-sitting for Sandy - until Patrick shows up.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Pet or Pests/ Komputer Overload
programme.desc: SpongeBob adopts a stray worm, which soon gives birth to a litter of baby worms.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Krabby Patty That Ate Bikini Bottom / Bubble Buddy Returns
programme.desc: Mr. Krabs uses Sandy's experimental growth formula on a Krabby Patty and the results are disastrous.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Big Sister Sam / Perfect Chemistry
programme.desc: Patrick's big sister Samantha comes for a visit; Sandy and Plankton bond over their shared love of science.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Extreme Sports/ Squirrel Record
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick try to join the extreme sports team the Drasticals. Also, Sandy finds a book of world's records and tries to break them all with SpongeBob's help.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Jellyfish Hunter
programme.desc: When money hungry Mr. Krabs discovers that customers love Spongebob's special jellyfish jelly Krabby Patty, he convinces Spongebob to bring him as many of the jellyfish as he can.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Squiditis/ Demolition Doofus
programme.desc: Gary will do anything to get the new treats SpongeBob brings home. Mr. Puff enters SpongeBob in the demolition derby.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Good Krabby Name/ Move It or Lose It
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick advertise the Krusty Krab. Then, petitions are circulated to see which restaurant will get bulldozed.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: License to Milkshake/ Squid Baby
programme.desc: SpongeBob learns his milkshake license has expired, and is forced to go back to the milkshake academy.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Karen 2.0/ Inspongeiac
programme.desc: Plankton's wife is replaced by a better version at work; Spongebob can't sleep.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: It Came From Goo Lagoon
programme.desc: A giant goo bubble emerges from Goo Lagoon and threatens all of Bikini Bottom. Plankton plans to use the bubble for his own evil ends.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Eek, an Urchin!/ Squid's Defense
programme.desc: Squidward wants SpongeBob and Sandy to teach him karate after being robbed of his groceries. SpongeBob goes to great lengths to force out a pesky sea urchin from the Krusty Krab.

programme.desc: Dipper's secret journal pages lead him and the gang to the author's hidden bunker where they find themselves face-to-face with a mysterious figure from Gravity Falls' lore.

programme.desc: On a family trip to Scrooge's ancestral castle, Dewey tries to hide the truth about his mom as his brothers search for family secrets.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Space Chicken/ Steak Night
programme.desc: To impress his new neighbors, Cricket attempts to launch a chicken into space. The Greens are excited to continue their traditional monthly steak dinner, but Cricket accidentally loses the steaks on the subway.

programme.title: Amphibia: Children of the Spore/ Anne of the Year
programme.desc: A scary mushroom adventure! Anne wins the prestigious “Frog of the Year” award and gets to plan her own party, but the frogs of Wartwood may not be ready for a human-style bash.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series: Drac to the Future
programme.desc: The self-updating tome, The Monstroso Serioso, is on display at Hotel Transylvania. While looking at the book, Kitty Cartwright finds herself sucked into its pages. It's up to Mavis to retrieve Kitty by travelling into the past.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series: Creepover Party/ Frankenstein and Son
programme.desc: Crabby’s first winning streak leads to a sponsorship deal… with Plunderman Industries? Later Ace creates a Scrammer using Sloane’s cherished childhood plaything… to disastrous results.

programme.title: Big Hero 6: The Series: Hardlight
programme.desc: Now fugitives, Big Hero 6 lay low as the SFPD searches for them. However, new villain Hardlight shows up hoping to challenge Big Hero 6 to a real-life videogame duel.

programme.title: Big Hero 6: The Series: Major Blast
programme.desc: Fred is targeted when a new villain, Major Blast appears in the city.

programme.title: Polaris: Player Select: Welcome to the Dungeon
programme.desc: A compilation of top gaming videos from YouTube stars.

programme.title: Parker Plays
programme.desc: YouTube star Parker Coppins shares his hilarious gameplay commentary.

programme.title: DuckTales: The Golden Spear!
programme.desc: As Della rebuilds her ship, Penumbra becomes convinced Della’s planning an attack. Meanwhile, Donald’s nap keeps getting interrupted by adventure.

programme.title: DuckTales: Nothing Can Stop Della Duck!
programme.desc: Della Duck has faced every danger the moon has to offer to reunite with her family, but her greatest challenge is one she might not be able to fight her way out of.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Boss Mabel
programme.desc: When Mabel questions the way Sam runs the Shack, he challenges her to a bet: whoever can make the most money in three days wins the chance to run the Shack however they wish for the rest of the summer.

programme.title: Amphibia: Croak and Punishment/ Trip to the Archives
programme.desc: When Sprig's Blue Moon Shell is stolen, he and Anne investigate to find the crook. To prepare for Anne's quest, the Plantars go to the archives and accidentally get trapped!

programme.title: DuckTales: Escape To/ From Atlantis
programme.desc: Dewey's attempts to impress Scrooge on a trip to the Lost City of Atlantis leads to trouble. Donald tries to protect the boys from his new employer who is trying to destroy them.

programme.title: DuckTales: The Impossible Summit of Mt. Neverrest!
programme.desc: Scrooge and Huey are determined to be the first to set foot atop an impossible summit, but the mountain holds a treacherous secret which had them test both their commitment and survival.

programme.title: Big Hero 6: The Series: Portal Enemy
programme.desc: Megan confronts Hiro about being a vigilante and threatens to tell Chief Cruz the truth.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Big Deal/ Forbidden Feline
programme.desc: Cricket persuades the family to hit up a Thanksgiving sale for a sweet deal on a new TV. Tilly hits it off with Anoosh, the neighborhood cat.

programme.title: Beyblade Burst Turbo: Bull’seye! Archer Hercules!
programme.desc: While Aiger is excited to face off against his friend, Hae-jin is torn between Blading and archery.

programme.title: Pokémon: Sun & Moon - Ultra Adventures: Bright Lights, Big Changes!

programme.title: The Closer: Waivers of Extradition
programme.desc: After the stabbing death of a woman who was apparently killed by someone wearing a ninja outfit, Brenda and the squad realize they might have a bigger case on their hands than they thought.

programme.title: The Closer: The Life
programme.desc: The squad investigates the murder of a 12-year-old boy found dead in a known gang party house. The case is soon complicated when two more bodies are found at a different yet related house. Meanwhile, trouble brews in Provenza's romantic paradise.

programme.title: The Closer: Make Over
programme.desc: Several old cases that were previously closed are suddenly re-opened after a crime lab is discredited. Provenza's former partner, Det. Andrews, comes out of retirement to help with a case involving artwork theft and murder.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Jung Frankenstein
programme.desc: Carrie tries to seductively coax Doug's therapist into targeting his many bad habits.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Attention Deficit
programme.desc: Doug's so busy planning his Super Bowl party that he forgets about Carrie's work problems.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Prints Charming
programme.desc: After accidentally picking up another couple's photos, Doug and Carrie feel that their lives are boring.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Animal Attraction
programme.desc: Doug has to transport live penguins to the zoo and has to cancel an evening with Carrie.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Golden Moldy
programme.desc: Doug ignores the mould problem in their house and spends the money on a vacation.

programme.title: The King of Queens: S'poor House

programme.title: The King of Queens: Steve Moscow

programme.title: The King of Queens: Cowardly Lyin'
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie try to be more open in their relationship after Doug is caught lying.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Buddhist Monk Murders
programme.desc: Several monks are found massacred at a Buddhist temple.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Profiles of intriguing crimes, accidents and outbreaks of disease from around the world are examined.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Footpath Murders
programme.desc: British detectives teamed up with a pioneering scientist to crack a case of sexual assault and serial murder. This 1986 case marked the first time DNA was used as evidence in a court of law.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Planted Evidence
programme.desc: A woman is found beaten, bound, strangled and possibly raped outside of Phoenix. The nearby plants tell investigators more about the killer than any other single piece of evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files: DNA Dragnet
programme.desc: Security camera video shows that what appears to be a casual encounter is actually a forced abduction, leading to murder. The perpetrator’s MO is remarkably similar to another murder which occurred five months earlier only 15 miles away.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Smiley Face
programme.desc: A woman is assaulted and stabbed nearly 30 times by an unknown assailant. Within days police hone in on a suspect who all but admits to the crime. As the trial approaches, DNA test results stun prosecutors and the suspect is released.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Second Shot at Love
programme.desc: A respected heart surgeon is found dead in the basement of his upscale Cincinnati home and have police assuming he committed suicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Journey to Justice
programme.desc: Prosecutor used video testimony to link drunk driver to hit-and-run which killed a child.

programme.title: Vengeance: Killer Coworkers: Lies, Cameras and Lingerie
programme.desc: When the entrepreneurial spirit turns two best friends into mortal enemies, one woman goes on the run.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sign of the Crime
programme.desc: Fire rips through a Seattle woman's apartment and some unsettling evidence is found in the rubble. Shortly after, a second fire breaks out just 100 feet from the first, and evidence points to a serial killer whose skills are evolving.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Cold Feet
programme.desc: A woman is abducted from her late-night job at a Florida convenience store. After she is found raped and strangled, investigators know the only way to pin down her killer will be through DNA analysis.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Shopping Spree
programme.desc: A mother and her daughter leave home for a day of shopping, but never return. The little girl's body was found dumped off of a roadside, but there was no sign of her mother. Forensic science determined if the baby's mother was a suspect or a victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Cheater
programme.desc: Little did Walter Scott of the band 'Bob Kuban and the In-Men' know that their 1966 hit-single 'The Cheater' would foreshadow the events that would lead to his demise.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Dirty Deed
programme.desc: Eileen and Derrick Severs disappeared from their home in the small village of Hambleton in Great Britain, and police found evidence which suggested foul play.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Blood Trail
programme.desc: In a quiet village in Great Britain, a farmer came upon a chilling sight. Impaled on his fence post was a severed lamb’s head along with a note which read, 'You next.'

programme.title: Forensic Files: Whodunit
programme.desc: An evening out to a murder mystery theatre turns into a real life whodunit.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Horse Play
programme.desc: A young, happily married woman died tragically in a horseback riding accident. Her husband seemed far from grief-stricken and, when they investigated his background, police discovered a web of lies and a proficiency with pharmaceuticals.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Missing Pearl
programme.desc: A woman doesn't return home, but a trail of blood is discovered leading to a basement.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Man's Best Friend?
programme.desc: When a young girl was found dead, her parents told police that she was killed by a dog.

programme.title: Vengeance: Killer Coworkers: Bloodshed in the Barn
programme.desc: Not long after taking a new job, beloved horse-trainer Fiona Southwell is found brutally murdered in a rural stable, and a small seaside town in Northern England must find the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Footpath Murders

programme.title: True Crime Live
programme.desc: Mike Galanos puts a spotlight on news and developments in criminal investigations.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sex, Lies and DNA
programme.desc: While Earl Morris was vacationing in California, he learned his wife had gone missing from their home in Arizona. The search for Ruby Morris involved dozens of investigators and scientists, even the coast guard.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Deadly Delivery
programme.desc: A serial bomber was waging a vendetta against the legal profession: A judge, two attorneys and even a courthouse had been targeted. Investigators used evidence from the bombs to lead them to the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Hell's Kitchen
programme.desc: The Kristine Fitzhugh Case - A woman is found dead in her home and it seems like an accidental fall down the stairs is to blame. When a pathologist discovers the death was no accident, police suspect it may have been a burglary gone wrong.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Three's a Crowd
programme.desc: As she left choir practice, the woman was gunned down in the church parking lot. Her husband became the prime suspect, particularly when police learned he found out just a month earlier that his wife had been cheating on him for three years.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Freeze Framed
programme.desc: An apparent suicide turns out to be a much more menacing scenario involving a black widow.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Touch of Evil
programme.desc: A dismembered body is dumped on the side of the New Jersey Turnpike and before police realize it, bodies are turning up between two states. A serial killer emerges, targeting Manhattan's gay community.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Wedding Cake Shine
programme.desc: Mark and Digger attempt their first backwoods miracle, turning wedding cake into moonshine. Tickle scrambles for cover when aerial surveillance threatens a return to jail. And Appalachia's most accident-prone shiner is back operating heavy machinery.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Drone vs. Shotgun
programme.desc: Josh breaks into his secret stash to build a new still site, and breaks out his shotgun when a drone invades his airspace. Daniel hits the jackpot selling for Mark and Digger, while Mike finds his steady customers mysteriously less thirsty than usual.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Busted by the Law
programme.desc: Tim devises a plan to make backwoods shine with Tickle for the first time in years. Short on patience and a key ingredient, Mark and Digger's bootleg operation comes under threat. In a series first, two Tennessee shiners get busted by cops on camera.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Toxic Work Environment
programme.desc: Tickle's carpentry skills shine as Virginia outlaws plan a risky mod to their still. Mark and Digger take a big swing on a small ingredient that can't pass the sniff test. Josh digs deep and uncovers the worst way to foster a toxic work environment.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Outlaw Cuts: Tickle to the Rescue
programme.desc: With Extra Shine and bonus scenes: Tickle helps Josh salvage both an abandoned submarine still and his season. Mark and Digger make bourbon from Indian corn while Van and Ewok gamble their freedom, risking jail time.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Run for the Hills
programme.desc: Mark and Digger attempt to rescue a former apprentice from a moonshine calamity. An unknown competitor invades Mike's turf, forcing him to change his shine strategy. Mark and Huck bank on backwoods survival skills to fuel their high-proof run.

programme.title: Guardians of the Glades: Choke Point
programme.desc: Dusty and team trek deep into an abandoned housing development in the Northern Glades where pythons are killing off wildlife. But when Dusty injures his foot on the hunt and is forced to turn back alone, he comes face to face with a monster snake.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Second Chances
programme.desc: After a disastrous first run Mark and Digger seek redemption on their 3-pot still. Tim enlists Tickle as JT's moonshine mentor. Patti and David build a second still in hopes of doubling their production and Lance tries to regain Jeff's trust running solo.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Run for the Hills
programme.desc: Mark and Digger attempt to rescue a former apprentice from a moonshine calamity. An unknown competitor invades Mike's turf, forcing him to change his shine strategy. Mark and Huck bank on backwoods survival skills to fuel their high-proof run.

programme.title: Guardians of the Glades: Choke Point
programme.desc: Dusty and team trek deep into an abandoned housing development in the Northern Glades where pythons are killing off wildlife. But when Dusty injures his foot on the hunt and is forced to turn back alone, he comes face to face with a monster snake.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Legends of the Shine
programme.desc: Mark and Digger test yeast strains to get the most out of a new shine recipe. Tim and JT visit moonshine legends. Mark Rodgers sets up a new still site and runs without a partner.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Pass the Juice
programme.desc: In North Carolina, Mike brings back an old family shine recipe. JT is eager to start running on his own. Mark and Digger try to troubleshoot a stalled fermentation while Patti and David deal with a deadly intruder at their still site.

programme.title: Building Alaska: Noah's Barge
programme.desc: Jere, Ben and Tim put the finishing touches on the biggest project they've undertaken: building a 112-foot floating barge. They struggle with transporting it to the water, and hurricane-force winds change their plans.

programme.title: Building Alaska: Stuck in Shelikof
programme.desc: Nick experiences firsthand just how dangerous living in Alaska can be while hunting for driftwood to build his fishing lodge and warehouse. Jere and Ben's 112-foot barge starts its half-mile journey to the water.

programme.title: Building Alaska: Rain, Rain, Go Away
programme.desc: Rain and a muddy trail continue to slow down Brannon's cabin build, but despite their dampened spirits, the gang is able to haul the windows across the two-mile trail. Jere and Ben finally get the barge launched.

programme.title: Building Alaska: Honey Bucket
programme.desc: Nick races to finish his cabin before his mom visits again. Bears are still a threat, so Nick makes sure the electric fence is working. Nick and Kent design and build a "honey bucket," also known as an Alaskan toilet.

programme.title: Building Alaska: Home Stretch
programme.desc: All the builders are in the home stretch in Alaska as they try to finish their cabins. Jere and Ben install interior and exterior siding. Nick's roof is dried in, so he turns to finishing the sleeping loft.

programme.title: Building Alaska: Grand Finale
programme.desc: Nick's dad, Paul, returns to help put the finishing touches on the cabin. Brannon's team builds countertops and railings for the cabin, and his client arrives. Jere and Ben put in hard work finishing the new Sea Otter Sound Lodge.

programme.title: Reclaimed: Seeking Ore
programme.desc: Alex and Kevin are modern prospectors who look for business opportunities on old mining claims. Homesteader Judd and his family will need their help to re-open the mine, and build three much needed weatherproof structures before winter hits.

programme.title: Reclaimed: Paydirt or Bust
programme.desc: Alex and Kevin are called to renovate a mine's machine shop for mining to begin generating income. With 57 veins that could average a profit of upwards of $250,000 a day, the guys must negotiate a deal that could be their biggest payoff yet.

programme.title: Reclaimed: Cabin in the Sky
programme.desc: Alex and Kevin are on a mission to help Ben and Laura build a cabin on their newly bought claim. The guys hope to negotiate for mining rights, but at 11,500 feet in altitude, the claim is nearly inaccessible. The only way up is by helicopter.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People: Wind and Water
programme.desc: The Brown Family kicks off their most ambitious plan to conquer the mountain yet. Bear and the siblings throw caution to the wind on a critical first project disassembling and transporting a salvaged 50-foot windmill.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People: Bears of a Feather
programme.desc: When a hungry bear breaks into Noah's tent and spooks the livestock, the Browns suspect it is being attracted by the ranch's newest arrivals, three young ostriches. Later, the windmill assembly takes the siblings to dangerous new heights.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People: Storm's Fury
programme.desc: The first major storm of the season threatens the family's progress. With a big bush wedding coming in days, the Browns race to obtain and haul much needed water tanks before the full force of the storm hits.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: We Don't Do Anything Normal
programme.desc: Bodybuilder Melissa's dress vision is simple: it must show off her muscles. Shay may lose his cool with Randy as they help Kat, a bride with a tight budget. Jasmine wants a unique bridal look to match her one-of-a-kind wedding on top of the Grand Canyon.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: I Want My Daughter to Look Like a Sexpot
programme.desc: Randy's mom visits for her 93rd birthday, and she's happy to share her fiery opinions with everyone shopping at Kleinfeld. Timid bride Courtney's appointment gets hijacked by her very provocative mother. Katie needs a gown suitable for two big secrets.

programme.title: My Big Fat Fabulous Life: Big Fat Moves
programme.desc: Whitney finally packs up and moves to Charlotte, forcing Buddy to find a new place to live. Meanwhile, Whitney teams up with Jessica for a fitness competition from hell.

programme.title: My Big Fat Fabulous Life: Friends with Benefits
programme.desc: Whitney's Greensboro friends come to Charlotte for a fun filled weekend. But things get tense when Heather discovers that Buddy's been staying with Whitney.

programme.title: I Am Jazz: I Will Survive
programme.desc: Jazz travels to New York for a third surgery to correct defects caused by complications during her gender confirmation surgery. While in New York, Jazz attends the premiere of a film she starred in. The Jennings family celebrate the twins' 21st birthday.

programme.title: My Big Fat Fabulous Life: Big Fat Moves
programme.desc: Whitney finally packs up and moves to Charlotte, forcing Buddy to find a new place to live. Meanwhile, Whitney teams up with Jessica for a fitness competition from hell.

programme.desc: Jazz travels to New York for a third surgery to correct defects caused by complications during her gender confirmation surgery. While in New York, Jazz attends the premiere of a film she starred in. The Jennings family celebrate the twins' 21st birthday.

programme.title: My Big Fat Fabulous Life: Big Fat Moves
programme.desc: Whitney finally packs up and moves to Charlotte, forcing Buddy to find a new place to live. Meanwhile, Whitney teams up with Jessica for a fitness competition from hell.

programme.title: My Big Fat Fabulous Life: We're Having a Baby
programme.desc: Whitney helps Ashley induce labour, but when push comes to shove, the labor coach panics. Meanwhile, Piggy bites Babs; Glenn announces a Hawaiian surprise that no one saw coming; and Heather gets a shocking letter from Buddy.

programme.title: My Big Fat Fabulous Life: A Sobering Reality
programme.desc: With the Hawaii trip fast approaching, Whitney prepares for the 5K. Whitney and Heather visit Buddy in rehab, and he tells the life and death story of how he got there.

programme.title: What Not to Wear: Addie
programme.desc: Addie is a 24 year-old mother and newspaper editor whose style is no match for her rocker fiancé. With her engagement party approaching, Stacy and Clinton are challenged to change this Austin mother's hippie ways.

programme.title: What Not to Wear: Serrita
programme.desc: Serrita is a Naval officer and mom who's off-duty attire brings sexy to submarine depths.

programme.title: The Tiniest Girl in the World
programme.desc: Charlotte is the tiniest girl in the world; follow along with her family as they try to get a diagnosis so they can plan their daughter's future.

programme.title: Help! I'm Turning Into a Giant
programme.desc: 31-year-old Tanya Angus suffers from Acromegaly, a rare condition that causes the body to grow uncontrollably. She is currently 6'6'', is 500lbs and still growing.

programme.title: Dr. Pimple Popper: This Cyst Persists
programme.desc: Half of Mary's body is a size 18, while the other half is a size 8. Anthony's cyst is in the area called the Triangle of Death. Shane wants to get a lump off his back. Brandi's anxiety could prevent her from getting a lump removed from her chest.

programme.title: Dr. Pimple Popper: Scared Cyst-Less
programme.desc: Taylore is a beautiful, young woman with unsightly elf-like bumps on her ears. Jose never goes out because of the baseball-sized lump on his head. Ken is covered in painful bumps that make it difficult to do everyday things.

programme.title: 1000-lb Sisters: A House Divided
programme.desc: After a stressful first month of dieting, the sisters head back to Atlanta for their one-month check in with Dr. Procter to find out if they are on the right path to their weight goals. The results force Amy to make a difficult decision.

programme.title: My 600-lb Life: Nikki's Story
programme.desc: Lifelong secret eater Nikki finds her life and career as a costume designer threatened by immobility as her weight climbs to 650 pounds. In urgent need of drastic weight loss, she undergoes gastric bypass and confronts the emotional causes for her eating.

programme.title: My 600-lb Life: Octavia's Story
programme.desc: Octavia has been bedbound for nearly a year when she decides to make the trip to Houston to get help from Dr. Now, but when it turns out that her best friend is also her worst enabler, Octavia will have to choose between old habits and a new life.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Rod and Ebony
programme.desc: Rob and Ebony fell in love after meeting on a dating website. After they met, though, Ebony revealed that she is transgendered.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Breana and Josh
programme.desc: Breana has waited to marry Josh for 11 years, but her love fades when the boys uncover the man behind the profile. Now, Josh's past catches up with him as Nev and Max launch a multistate manhunt to uncover the dark truth.

programme.title: Siesta Key: New man, who dis?
programme.desc: Madisson's father disapproves of her much older man. Cara is apprehensive to make a clean break with Garrett. Juliette's romance leaves Chloe questioning Robby, leading to a huge confrontation between Juliette and Chloe.

programme.title: Siesta Key: Where's My Apology?
programme.desc: Juliette is isolated from everyone when Chloe threatens legal action. Garrett and Cara continue to struggle with their relationship. Madisson doubts Amanda's intentions with Brandon. Jared and Kelsey explore their chemistry.

programme.title: Siesta Key: Are you offended? Because you should be!
programme.desc: Juliette has to choose between her new job or going to Greece with her new boyfriend, Robby. Alex makes a play for a newly single Cara. Brandon and Amanda's budding romance is threatened by Madisson and Amanda's ex, JJ.

programme.title: Teen Mom: Young and Pregnant: Looking for Signs
programme.desc: The guy Brianna's been talking to online makes a move. Ashley reaches a big milestone. Rachel wants her dad to meet her baby. Kayla's mom makes a decision that affects everyone. Kiaya has very high expectations for Teazha's prom.

programme.title: Ridiculousness: Chief Keef
programme.desc: Rob, Chanel and Steelo welcome rap superstar Chief Keef and check out some people who are 'back from the dead', take an off-road ride for some 'four-wheel fun', and voice some opinions with 'I don't like'.

programme.title: Ridiculousness: Wale
programme.desc: Rob, Steelo and Chanel hang out with Hip-Hop superstar Wale, meet a few women with 'no chill', see who's 'numb to the madness', and meet some 'fans of the district'.

programme.title: Ridiculousness: Chanel and Sterling XXVI
programme.desc: Rob, Chanel and Sterling check out some hard working monkeys in 'bananas ain't free,' throw down in 'stripe club' and dress up nice for some 'formal slams.'

programme.title: Ridiculousness: Jay Rock
programme.desc: Rob, Chanel and Steelo welcome rapper Jay Rock as they expose some "Fakers," check out some people who've been "Slammed Straight," and find out that there's no escaping "Karma Kraze."

programme.title: Ridiculousness: Chanel and Sterling LIX
programme.desc: Rob, Chanel and Steelo salute the troops in Training Basics, discover a new sport called Drunkour, and witness the majesty of Hobo Wizards.

programme.title: Ridiculousness: Sage the Gemini
programme.desc: Rob, Chanel and Sterling have rapper and producer Sage the Gemini on the show to find out if he's 'glide or die,' get 'over gassed' and announce to the world 'I love sparks.'

programme.title: Ridiculousness: Chanel and Sterling XXXIV
programme.desc: Rob, Chanel and Sterling take the 'Running Man Challenge,' explore the fear known as 'Crap-nophobia' and learn what it means to be 'Saved to Death.'

programme.title: Ridiculousness: Rockdiculousness With Steel Panther
programme.desc: The 80s make a comeback when Steel Panther joins Rob, Chanel and Sterling to ignite the Flame Fuel, take some Community Property and prepare to be Livin' on a Prayer.

programme.title: Ridiculousness: Chanel and Sterling XXVIII
programme.desc: Rob, Chanel and Sterling discuss a condition called "Temporary Fansanity," people who are "Quick To Cry" and our favorite summer sporting event, the "Bro Games."

programme.title: Ridiculousness: Bella Twins
programme.desc: Twin divas Nicole and Brianna stop by the set to show some of their backyard wrestling moves and how to make a perfect pain angel.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Antoinette and Albert
programme.desc: When Antoinette meets a young rapper, the cyber attraction turns serious. After a year of talking on the phone and texting countless emoticons, Antoinette wants to take things to the next level.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Traves and Candy
programme.desc: Traves hopes for help with his internet girlfriend, Candy, which seems like a regular catfish. As the guys begin to learn more and more, they quickly realize there is far more to this catfishing story than it appears.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: April and Dean
programme.desc: April and Dean have been chatting online for over seven years, since April was 13. Every now and then, Dean would seemingly disappear online for months at a time. Nev and Max want to help April track Dean down before his next disappearance.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Chelsea and Lennie
programme.desc: Nev and co-host Tallulah Willis help Chelsea, a young woman who’s fallen for a reality TV star. But digging for the truth only reveals Chelsea has her own secrets.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Jesse and Brian
programme.desc: When Jesse met Brian on Facebook, she thought she'd hit the jackpot. They made plans to meet, but Brian stood her up. Now, years later, Brian has asked her to live with him, but she can't stop wondering what he's hiding.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Telizza and Shai
programme.desc: 18-year-old Telizza became bi-curious when her online friendship with Shai turned romantic. Frustrated by several failed attempts to video chat, Telizza has turned to Nev and Max to uncover the truth.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Dallas and Safari
programme.desc: Instafamous model Dallas has his sights set on sexy Safari ever since she slid into his DMs – but despite flirty promises, she won’t meet.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Kelsie and Brandon
programme.desc: Kelsie is deeply infatuated with online love Brandon, but his reluctance to meet up over the years has Kelsie reaching out to Nev and Max.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Nique and Alice
programme.desc: Co-host popstar Elle King joins Nev to help Nique find her Tumblr love, Alice. Digging deep leads to more questions than answers as Nev and Elle have concerns about who Alice could be, and what her true intentions are with Nique.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Artis and Jess
programme.desc: After meeting while in other relationships, Artis is ready to meet Jess.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Trina and Scorpio
programme.desc: Trina is an exotic dancer who always had trouble meeting decent guys.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Cherie and Avion
programme.desc: Sexy Avion swayed Cherie to send him explicit photos, but Cherie's worried she has trusted the wrong man with her pics. Nev and Justin Combs try to help, but when they get an angry call from Avion's wife, things go south fast!

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Jamey and Ari
programme.desc: Jamey fell for Ari, a fellow Texan he met online. Jamey will do anything to meet her, but Ari says she will only meet him in-person on TV. With the help of Nev and Max, Ari shares her story and hopes Jamey will accept her.

programme.title: Chopped: A Frog Leg Up

programme.title: Chopped: Leftover Takeover

programme.title: Chopped: Weird Twist

programme.title: Chopped: Just Add Cheese!
programme.desc: Comfort food connoisseurs and gourmands alike know that any dish can benefit from cheese. In this special competition, every basket is filled with cheesy yumminess including an unusual take on a Philadelphia classic in the first round.

programme.title: Chopped: Take the Cake
programme.desc: In the first round, the chefs learn they must make appetizers that include ginger-like galangal and a trendy taco. Miniature fruit and softshell crabs are two of the surprises that the competitors face in the entree round.

programme.title: Chopped: $50,000 Champs Challenge: Part 2

programme.title: Chopped: B.L.D.: Breakfast, Lunch and Dinner!
programme.desc: The chefs set out to make a whole day's worth of amazing food when the cooking rounds are changed to breakfast, lunch and dinner. The judges hope the chefs who make it to the final round will be able to make lamb dinners to remember.

programme.title: Chopped: Tailgate Greats
programme.desc: Four devoted football fans are super proud of their can't-fail tailgating games, but in the Chopped Kitchen, only one can become a champ. Handy sandwiches and sweet popcorn are two of the ingredients the competitors face in the first round.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Waste Not, Want Not
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is reclaiming kitchen scraps and transforming them into star dishes. Peels, stems and bones destined for the trash are instead turned into crispy roasted potato peels, stir fried broccoli stems and an awesome all-purpose chicken broth.

programme.title: Farmhouse Rules: The Bowlerinas
programme.desc: Nancy prepares cream cheese stuffed French toast and sausage bacon hash with baked tomatoes.

programme.title: Farmhouse Rules: From Farmhouse to Firehouse
programme.desc: Today, Nancy Fuller is bringing the farmhouse to the firehouse with her food. She's whipping up a spicy meal to say thank you to the local men and women who serve the community. She makes her spicy three-meat chili, jalapeno cornbread and creamed kale.

programme.title: Farmhouse Rules: Treasures in the Attic
programme.desc: Nancy cleans out the attic with her two granddaughters, searching for hidden treasures to pass down. They then feast on a delicious meal of homemade orange marmalade and hand-rolled whole grain crackers.

programme.title: Farmhouse Rules: Farmer's Feast
programme.desc: Nancy Fuller is hosting a dinner for the hardworking farmers of the Farm Bureau, who volunteer to promote agriculture in the New York area. She honors their work by creating a succulent meal of Devils on the Water, Shortrib Stew with Port and Horseradish.

programme.title: Farmhouse Rules: Teaching Little Men to Cook 101
programme.desc: Nancy Fuller is teaching her grandson, Miles, and a few of his friends how to cook! They're itchin' to get in the kitchen, so she shows them the way as they cook foolproof chicken parmesan, spaghetti and meatballs, and sauteed green beans.

programme.title: Farmhouse Rules: Canvas and Cocktails
programme.desc: Nancy Fuller's good friend is teaching a canvas and cocktails class, so she's promised to teach Nancy to paint in exchange for drinks. Nancy goes all in with a full-on meal that includes lemon and oregano chicken, orzo pilaf salad and mustard greens.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Ranch Games
programme.desc: The family that plays together stays together. Ree, Ladd and the extended family get together for some good ol' friendly ranch style sporting competition. Afterwards, there's tangy tomato brisket, onion strings, and cheesy, blanched broccoli.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: First Time for Everything
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is taking time out to whip up some amazing dishes she's never tried before, such as falafel with chipotle tahini dip piled into a pita. Next, she gives chicken and waffles a shot, with a decadent maple syrup and bourbon sauce.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Five-Star Showdown
programme.desc: Guy Fieri transforms Flavortown into a high-end restaurant and invites four chefs for three rounds of 5-star cooking. First he challenges them to elevate the ingredients in a frozen TV dinner, then to prepare signature dishes.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Big Burger Battle
programme.desc: Chefs who claim they make perfect patties compete in an all burger edition. First they must create an international burger using only eight items from the express lane. The two finalists must shop quickly as the market closes early.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Adventure Headquarters

programme.desc: Pete treks to New York's Catskill Mountains to build an NTS first - a glasshouse in the sky. Piecing materials together like a puzzle, Pete uses his clients' collection of 50 vintage windows to construct an elegant treehouse encased by walls of glass.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: International: France
programme.desc: Pete travels through France with treehouse visionary, Alain Laurens. Winding through the magical countryside, Alain shows Pete his fantastical structures that implement ingenious engineering, reach dizzying heights and redefine what a treehouse can be.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Super Spy Treehouse

programme.desc: Pete and his crew are building a pair of treehouses that can accommodate everyone in the family, including their pet pooches.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Barnwood Cottage
programme.desc: The guys turn a brand new storage shed into an 1800s log cabin, then drive it to Round Top, Texas. Mark gets a lesson from a local silversmith, and the team visits historic structures built around the state by 19th-century pioneers.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: The Finished Product
programme.desc: Mark Bowe visits finished cabins he helped build in Montana, Texas, South Carolina, West Virginia and North Carolina. Then, there's a big surprise waiting for him in Gatlinburg, Tennessee.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet: Safe from the Storm

programme.title: The Vet Life: No Goats, No Glory
programme.desc: While Dr. Blue and Dr. Ross help a client at their goat farm, Dr. Lavigne must save a pup with an obstruction in his intestines and a concerned family waiting. Dr. Blue and Dr. Ross compete in a cook-off to see who the best grill master is.

programme.title: The Zoo: The Marvelous Mott Mott
programme.desc: Max, a beloved camel, develops a large abscess on the top of his head. An adorable marmot prepares for her first classroom visit as an ambassador animal. Later, a peregrine falcon found at the base of skyscraper is bought to the zoo for a health check.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Like a Boss

programme.desc: A firefighter and his wife are looking for a Bahamian getaway on Long Island where they can escape the relentless traffic in Los Angeles. She has her heart set on a relaxing retreat, but he and the kids want to be close to the action and adventure.

programme.title: Bahamas Life: Turning a Picture into Reality
programme.desc: A Pennsylvania couple who've been staring at a picture of a beach house on their fridge for years decide to take a leap and make that picture their reality.

programme.title: Home Town: So Long Loft, Hello Home
programme.desc: A family of four has outgrown their loft and is ready to use their all-in budget of $200,000 to move into an family home with a yard. Ben and Erin show the parents two family-friendly houses, including a dated, boxy home with 14 acres of property.

programme.title: Vintage Flip: Jessie and Tina Revive a 1924 Spanish Revival
programme.desc: Jessie and Tina acquire a Spanish-style home just blocks from picturesque downtown Monrovia, California. However, this home's Spanish character is completely lackluster.

programme.title: Vintage Flip: 1950s Ranch
programme.desc: Jessie and Tina are restoring an abandoned ranch home in West Covina, CA. The home was built in 1952 during Southern California's car culture boom, and they are excited to channel a vintage car theme in the home's design.

programme.title: Flip or Flop Fort Worth: Foundation for a Flip
programme.desc: Andy and Ashley flip an adorable ranch home in a desirable suburb.

programme.title: Flip or Flop Fort Worth: Pulling Out a Paycheck
programme.desc: Andy and Ashley take on flipping a beat-down house in the desirable suburb of Burton Hills. Once they break ground, their plan to turn a nasty outdoor patio into a luxurious master bedroom is foiled when city permitting shuts them down.

programme.title: Desert Flippers: Uneasy Street
programme.desc: Lindsey and Eric ignore the 'location, location, location' credo of real estate and flip a house on a busy road. The dated 90s style house gets revived as an open-concept dual-master retreat. The stunning remodel might not be enough in the noisy location.

programme.title: Desert Flippers: Five-bedroom Flip
programme.desc: Lindsey and Eric find a five-bedroom home with a list price well below the area comps. Although the property looks good on the outside, the interior is a mess that was left in a state of mid-renovation.

programme.title: Fixer Upper: Behind the Design: The Morrison House
programme.desc: Joanna helps a family turn their outdated ranch home into a French country retreat. She uses soft neutral colors, lots of texture, natural wood tones and floor-to-ceiling windows to blend the existing traditional elements with the new.

programme.title: Fixer Upper: Behind the Design: The Sandvall House
programme.desc: Joanna Gaines and her design team take on the Mountain House, a 1980s-era home in Woodway that's lacking character and charm.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: From Studs to Spindles
programme.desc: Tamara Day steps in to finish the renovation of a 1890s Craftsman-style home. This home was previously ripped down to the studs by a contractor before it was abandoned.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Book Lamps and Bubble Glass
programme.desc: Renovations on the 1890s Craftsman-style home continues as Tamara enlarges the master bedroom and creates a beautiful study. She runs into trouble when the antique tiles for the bathroom floor are not salvageable. Tamara finds a way to re-use old glass.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: The Mural of the Story
programme.desc: Tamara works on restoring a 1909 Craftsman home, ripping out the tacky additions that have stripped it of its original charm. She saves a wallpaper mural that could be the showpiece of the dining room, if she can keep it from being destroyed.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Clowning Around
programme.desc: Tamara continues with the 1909 Craftsman, transforming a small bedroom into the master bath. She saves and refurbishes French doors, vintage sinks, and a tub from the house, while adding modern elements like a sauna.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Move That Kitchen
programme.desc: Tamara renovates a single-story ranch and tackles her biggest challenge to date, transforming the former master bedroom into a stunning, open kitchen. However, she and her dad run into problems during demolition.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Copper Showstopper
programme.desc: Tamara continues renovations on the ranch by creating a new master suite. While polishing the copper fireplace hood, Tamara and her dad use a familiar solution that has unusual results. Her plans to create a sunroom run into problems due to weather.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Rhapsody in Beige
programme.desc: Tamara transforms a contemporary home on a large wooded lot that's devoid of any character. She installs huge windows to bring the outdoors inside, but she faces issues with resurfacing a fireplace veneer.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: A Pain in the Deck
programme.desc: Continuing renovations on a large and bland contemporary home, Tamara tackles the master suite and replaces lots of dated cabinetry to make a stylish bathroom. She also creates a home heater and an immense deck and patio.

programme.title: Buying and Selling With the Property Brothers: Letting Go Is Hard to Do
programme.desc: A couple wants to downsize and is eager to sell before a subdivision floods their neighborhood with brand new homes, but their outdated property is making a lousy first impression.

programme.title: Buying and Selling With the Property Brothers: Plans To Expand

programme.title: Buying and Selling With the Property Brothers: Making the Most of Nana's Gift
programme.desc: A couple has outgrown their bachelor pad and is desperate for more space. Thanks to a generous gift from Grandma, the couple is prepared to undertake an ambitious overhaul.

programme.title: Buying and Selling With the Property Brothers: Stuck in a Suburban Starter

programme.title: Counting Cars: Soap Box
programme.desc: Danny and Horny Mike get too close for comfort when they convince a Model-T Hot Rod owner to let them take it for a spin. Later, a Soap Box race car brings out the inner child in Roli and Mike.

programme.title: Counting Cars: You Talkin' to Me?
programme.desc: A Hollywood icon confronts Horny Mike and Roli in the shop parking lot. Danny tries to diffuse the situation before it gets explosive.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Not So Pretty in Pink
programme.desc: The shop agrees to build a brave father a hot rod wheelchair, and Danny gets stuck in a bind when a pink car project doesn't go as planned.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Size Matters
programme.desc: Danny's friend Brian has always wanted to ride a motorcycle, but has never been able due to his physical limitations. Will Danny and the guys be able to get Brian in the wind for the first time?

programme.title: Counting Cars: To Die For
programme.desc: Danny finds are rare 1960 delivery vehicle to show off, and the guys build a coffin couch.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Pimpmobile
programme.desc: The shop finally gets to work on Danny's famous '71 Super Fly Cadillac, but Kevin isn't feeling the love. Later, Grandpa wins big at poker, but blows a deal for a new motorcycle.

programme.title: Counting Cars: The Great Car Hunt
programme.desc: Danny sees something in his shop that makes him very uncomfortable: emptiness. Danny sends the entire crew to the streets to find him cars but promises that he will be the one to return with the biggest and baddest catch of the day.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Pimp My Bus
programme.desc: Kevin disapproves of how much money Danny is spending tricking out his dream FMC Motorhome, but Danny has a surprise plan to help him get over it. Later, he builds a movie replica bike to commemorate a customer's lost friend.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Roadrunner Recon
programme.desc: Danny attempts to create a badass muscle car from a 4-door sedan, and a one of a kind couch from the tail end of a Plymouth Roadrunner.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Danny's Dream, Kevin's Nightmare
programme.desc: Danny has wanted a Plymouth Roadrunner ever since a couple of car nuts pulled him over a few years ago and bought his Sebring on the spot. He's finally found one and he's doing it up exactly as he wants to. Meanwhile, Horny Mike takes on a project.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Burning Out
programme.desc: Danny has big retro plans for a 1972 Cutlass he hopes to flip, and is counting on his fab team to do her right. Roli seeks the help of the guys to learn to do a burn-out. Danny checks out a 1971 Torino whose owner can't seem to put a price on it.

programme.title: Counting Cars: '70s Scoot
programme.desc: Danny takes on a psychedelic chopper project that is so '70s cool, he's worried he might not be able to give it to the customer when it's done. Later, he hits the streets to check out a pair of classic Mini Coopers and a rare Split Bumper Camaro.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Swap Meet Mayhem
programme.desc: When Danny challenges the guys to come up with innovative money-making ideas for the shop, Shannon proposes building a bike from parts picked up at a swap meet, and Mike ups the ante by challenging Shannon to do it all in one day.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Blinged Out Blazer
programme.desc: Danny's got himself a new ride, a classic two-wheel drive Chevy Blazer that he wants to trick out and flip for a big profit. Painter Ryan wants to try out a new twist on a retro flame design, but Danny is worried that it will look too 'Miami Vice.'

programme.title: Counting Cars: Bonneville Bound Part 1
programme.desc: The Turbinator, the vehicle holding a land speed record, needs a paint job to make it look as fast as it goes. Danny decides the crew should be there in Bonneville, Utah when it goes for the speed record again.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Bonneville Bound Part 2
programme.desc: Ryan and Kevin go to the Bonneville Salt Flats to see The Turbinator try to break 500 MPH. Back at the shop, Davey Deals asks Danny to help him sell a classic 1951 Nash, and Mike and Paul trick out an electric bike.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Patriotic Chopper
programme.desc: Danny and the team are commissioned to build a bike for a true American hero, a military veteran. He wants to show off his love for family and country by covering the bike with American flags, and portraits of loved ones who also served.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Big Truck Custom Combo
programme.desc: Danny is commissioned to custom paint a brand new pick-up for a charity auction for the local YMCA. But Danny can't stop at paint, he adds the ultimate accessory a matching chopper that fits in the back.

programme.desc: While out on a bank run for his off-duty security job, Halstead and his co-workers find themselves under attack by a violent robbery crew which quickly becomes a full-scale shoot-out.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: In a Duffle Bag
programme.desc: Voight responds to a call about a suspicious duffle bag, and uncovers a frigid newborn baby. Determined to find the culprit who abandoned the infant, Intelligence receives a promising lead based on information provided by Chicago Med.

programme.desc: After a hooded man opens fire on their patrol car, Roman is left wounded and Burgess takes off after the shooter. No gun is found at the crime scene and the victim is a 17-year-old honour student who will likely never walk again.

programme.desc: How long does it take a killer to stop worrying if they'll ever be caught. If they're still free then maybe they got away with murder. The trail goes cold, never to warm up again until one day it does.

programme.title: Mom: Spaghetti Sauce and a Dumpster Fire

programme.title: Mom: Ocular Fluid and Fighting Robots

programme.title: Secrets of the Zoo: Hoofin' It
programme.desc: It’s a busy day at The Wilds. Keepers perform a gnarly hoof trim and race to lower a painted dog’s spiked temperature.

programme.title: Secrets of the Zoo: Doe a Deer
programme.desc: With so many newborns arriving, it’s baby boom time at The Wilds. And a camel with a bloated belly has keepers worried.

programme.title: Secrets of the Zoo: The Wild Side: A Horse, Of Course
programme.desc: Rhino Anan is due for an ultrasound and Prezawalski horse Flicka gets feisty during an eye exam.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol: Storm's a-Mooin'
programme.desc: When a massive flood strikes Michigan, humans and animals alike are reeling from the devastating effects, making it a wet and wild week for the vets.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol: The Fourth of Pol-ly!
programme.desc: From lightning strikes to cat fights, the clinic is seeing a slew of interesting cases.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol: One Pol Over the Cuckoo's Nest
programme.desc: It's a chirpy and chipper set of cases for the vets of Pol Veterinary Services as they deal with ornery ungulates, cut colts, knobby knees and more.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol: Mooovin' on Up
programme.desc: Dr. Pol and team rush to get their patients healed before the Isabella County Fair.

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost: Egypt's Atlantis: The New Treasures
programme.desc: After experts discover an ancient lost city off the coast of Egypt, the race to unearth its priceless relics and bring this mysterious sunken world back to life begins, revealing the doomsday event that caused it to sink into the sea.

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost: Secrets of the Alien Relic
programme.desc: A strange, alien artifact discovered in the ruins of an ancient Greek shipwreck holds the secrets of a technology thousands of years ahead of its time. Now experts are using cutting-edge science to reveal what this mysterious device was used for.

programme.title: The Last Days of Pompeii
programme.desc: New archaeological technology reveals the doomed city of Pompeii as it existed during its final hours, and using cutting-edge science, experts uncover the long-lost secrets and treasures buried by the ravages of apocalypse.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Forbidden History tells true stories about treasure, conspiracies, and lost civilizations.

programme.title: Forbidden History: Hitler's Executioner
programme.desc: We reveal the remarkable story of Johann Reichhart, the man who went from German state executioner before WW2 to head up a merciless Nazi killing machine which murdered thousands. In a bizarre twist of fate, Reichhart ended up working for the Allies.

programme.title: Unearthed: Finding Sparta's 300
programme.desc: New discoveries might lead investigators to the legendary group of 300 Spartan warriors who died fighting off one million invading Persians, and using cutting-edge tech, experts reconstruct the lost battlefield to uncover the secrets of their last stand.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Forbidden History tells true stories about treasure, conspiracies, and lost civilizations.

programme.title: Forbidden History: Hitler's Executioner
programme.desc: We reveal the remarkable story of Johann Reichhart, the man who went from German state executioner before WW2 to head up a merciless Nazi killing machine which murdered thousands. In a bizarre twist of fate, Reichhart ended up working for the Allies.

programme.title: Unearthed: Lost City of Troy
programme.desc: The legendary lost city at the center of the Trojan War is Troy, but its existence has never been confirmed. Now, discoveries and cutting-edge science might prove if this place existed, and if the Greeks really conquered it with the infamous Trojan Horse.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Riddle in the Ruins
programme.desc: Strange black holes appear near Egypt's ancient pyramids, and new evidence points to a disturbing conclusion. Ruins in Peru suggests that the Inca people knew far more than previously thought. A mysterious red swarm invades a beach in India.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Ghost of Zorro
programme.desc: A giant letter Z is carved into a Mexican hillside, evoking the mark of a legendary outlaw. A strange sighting in Germany points to secrets from the Cold War. Massive, blood-red marks in China prompt fears of a violent government crackdown.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Finding Jack the Ripper
programme.desc: New discoveries from space give experts new clues in the hunt for Jack the Ripper. When a strange object crash-lands in Antarctica, experts speculate it could be an alien visitor.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Forbidden City of Inca Gold
programme.desc: Treasure hunters have searched for a lost Incan city of untold wealth for a century, but new technology might finally locate its riches. A strange mountain in Egypt sparks an investigation into Cleopatra's gems and the tomb of Alexander the Great.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Cannibals in the Ghost City
programme.desc: An abandoned city in the African jungle might have a bloody past of malevolent spirits and cannibals. Mount Vesuvius shows signs that it's awakening, and the mafia could be to blame. Experts investigate Hitler's secret Nazi UFO program.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Abandoned: Nazi Ghost Town
programme.desc: Revisit a Soviet megastructure, a massive Nazi brainwashing complex, and a dangerous desert pass to find out how they were built and why they were mysteriously abandoned.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Abandoned: World's Strangest Ghost Towns
programme.desc: The world's strangest ghost towns are home to dark secrets, creepy buildings, and eerie sights, and new discoveries reveal why they were abandoned.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Abandoned: Soviet Doomsday Device
programme.desc: Explore what happened to a classified Soviet machine built to prevent all-out nuclear war, a top secret Nazi facility, and a strange tower that left an enduring legacy.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Abandoned: Nightmare on Blood Mountain
programme.desc: A ghost ship thousands of miles away from where anyone expected, a bridge to nowhere in Florida's Hurricane Alley, and a haunting mountain complex with a bloody secret are deserted engineering projects, and new discoveries reveal why they were abandoned.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Abandoned: Curse of Hell Island
programme.desc: Explore a ghost town that was once a dangerous industrial hellscape, a giant warship at the bottom of the ocean, and a top secret relic that could have changed the Cold War.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Abandoned: America's Doomsday Town
programme.desc: A strange deserted city in America's heartland, an eerie gigantic wall deep in snow-capped mountains, and a mysterious top-secret Soviet facility were built and then abandoned.

programme.title: Female Forces: For Better or for Worse
programme.desc: Officer Dawn Yorke tries to settle domestic dispute with a wife who refuses to let her abusive husband back into their home. Next, Sgt. Betsy Brantner-Smith gives a good lecture to a husband who gets pulled over after bickering with his wife.

programme.desc: Rookie officer Sara Rolfe completes her police training and gets a surprise visit from Betsy and Julie when they drop by the academy to check on her progress. Sara then gets a taste of real police work when she goes on patrol.

programme.desc: Plenty of tears are shed by suspects and officers; Betsy escorts a war veteran.

programme.desc: Like any dad, Titus wants the best for his son. As an EMT, he sees just how dire life can get. Meanwhile, police officers continue to work desperately to get guns off the street and make the city a safer place to live.
